title: Sections & Content
intro: How page content organises into sections, cards, and nested groups. One spacing hierarchy — 48 / 32 / 24 / 16 / 4 — governs every container, and every section header follows the same recipe.

blocks.title: The laws of sections
blocks.intro: These rules are non-negotiable. Every page, drawer, and panel in the product follows the same containment model.

items.rule: Padding steps down one level per nesting depth — 48 / 32 / 24 / 16 / 4, never flat.
items.why: Uniform padding at every depth erases containment; the step-down is what makes parent/child structure legible at a glance.

items.rule: A container's padding is never smaller than the gap between its children.
items.why: When padding undercuts the child gap, content sits closer to the frame than to its siblings and the grouping collapses.

items.rule: Section headers are one fixed recipe: `h4`/600 title, optional `p2`/medium subtitle, then a 1px #e8e8e8 divider 12px below the text and 24px above the content.
items.why: Heading drift — different sizes, weights, or missing dividers per section — destroys the scan rhythm of the page.

items.rule: Major sections stack on a 32px (`--space-6`) rhythm.
items.why: A section gap larger than any intra-section spacing is the signal that separates topics from items.

items.rule: Card anatomy is fixed: 1px #e8e8e8 border, 8px radius, `overflow: hidden`, #fafafa header at 16px padding, body at 24px.
items.why: The tighter header against the roomier body guides the eye from label to content; ad-hoc paddings break that contrast.

items.rule: Nested sub-sections step down: 16px padding, 6px radius, lighter #f0f0f0 border.
items.why: Same-weight borders at two depths read as siblings, not parent and child.

items.rule: Dividers separate sections; spacing alone separates items within a section.
items.why: A ruled line signals a topic change — between fields it only adds noise.

items.rule: Layout flows through `Vertical`/`Horizontal` props (`gap`, `flex`, `height`, `justifyContent`), never inline flex styles.
items.why: Style overrides bypass the layout API, so spacing can no longer be audited against the token scale.

blocks.title: Nesting hierarchy
blocks.caption: The 48 / 32 / 24 / 16 / 4 step-down visualised as nested containers — page canvas, section gap, card padding, subsection padding, field gap, label-input — with the token quick-reference below.

blocks.title: Section headers
blocks.caption: The fixed header recipe with and without a subtitle: h4/600 title, p2/medium subtitle, 1px divider 12px below the text and 24px above the content.

blocks.title: Section card anatomy
blocks.caption: Bordered card with shaded 16px header and 24px body, plus the structure-to-token mapping breakdown (header → space-4, body → space-5, field gaps → space-4, label-input → space-1).

blocks.title: Nested sub-sections
blocks.caption: Parent card at 24px body padding containing 16px children with the lighter #f0f0f0 border — the one-step padding drop that makes nesting read as containment.

blocks.title: Dividers vs spacing
blocks.caption: Divider + 32px gap between major sections, and spacing-only 16px gaps between fields in a group — no divider lines. The full when-to-use-which lists live in the decision table below.

blocks.title: Do / don't pairs
blocks.caption: Stepped padding vs flat 12px everywhere; consistent header recipe vs mixed heading levels with no dividers.

blocks.title: Spacing scale
blocks.intro: Memorise the step-down: 48 > 32 > 24 > 16 > 4. Each level is visually contained within its parent.

rows.use: Page canvas padding — the outermost frame
Card header padding, sub-section padding, field gaps
Card borders, section dividers, card header border-bottom
Nested sub-section borders — one shade lighter than the parent card

blocks.title: Canonical section skeleton
blocks.code:
// One page = stacked sections on a 32px rhythm
{/* Section header — fixed recipe */} Pricing Configuration Base rates and adjustments for this contract.
{/* Section card */}
Contract Details
{/* Nested sub-section: one step down */}
Base Rates
{/* fields — label sits 4px above its input */}
blocks.note: Gaps and flex go through Vertical/Horizontal props, never style. Texto categories used here (h4, p1, p2) are all valid; there is no p3 or h6.

blocks.title: Section formats
blocks.intro: Three containment levels, one decision: how much framing does the content need?

rows.name: Bare section header
rows.when: Default for page-level structure. Title + divider, content sits directly on the canvas — the canvas itself is the container.

rows.name: Section card
rows.when: Related fields or mixed content that needs explicit containment: 1px border, 8px radius, shaded 16px header, 24px body.

rows.name: Nested sub-section
rows.when: Sub-grouping inside a card body. One step down: 16px padding, 6px radius, #f0f0f0 border, p2/600 title in a 10px 16px header bar.

items.do: Step padding down inward: card header 16px, body 24px, sub-sections 16px, field gaps 16px.
items.dont: Flatten everything to one value — 12px on header, body, sub-sections, and gaps alike.
items.why: Stepped padding creates visual containment; flat padding makes content feel uncontained and hard to scan.

items.do: Give every section the identical header recipe and a uniform 32px gap between sections.
items.dont: Mix heading levels per section — h4 here, an oversized p1 there, a small p2 next, some with dividers and some without.
items.why: A predictable rhythm lets readers locate sections without reading them.

items.do: Rule a divider below section headers and between major content blocks, card groups, and tab content areas.
items.dont: Draw dividers between form fields, list items, or paragraphs — 16px spacing already separates them.
items.why: Lines accumulate into clutter; spacing is invisible structure.

blocks.title: Divider decision table
blocks.body:
Use a divider: between page sections, between card groups, below section headers, between tab content areas.

Use spacing only: between form fields, between list items, between paragraphs, between inline elements.

items.title: Texto has no p3 or h6
items.detail: Valid categories are p1, p2, label, heading, heading-small, and h1-h5 only. Sub-section titles use `category='p2' weight='600'`, not a smaller heading category.

items.title: appearance='secondary' is blue, not gray
items.detail: The muted subtitle under a section title uses `appearance='medium'`. `secondary` maps to the brand blue and reads as a link.

items.title: overflow: hidden is load-bearing on cards
items.detail: The card wrapper clips its #fafafa header fill to the 8px radius. Drop `overflow: 'hidden'` and the header paints square corners over the rounded border.

items.title: Don't stack borders at container seams
items.detail: A card's border-top must not sit flush against a wrapper that also draws border-top — the doubled 2px line reads as a rendering bug. One element owns each seam.
